text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashings. In today's video, we are going to discuss various types of learnings in AI or you can also say it as various types of machine learnings. So here if we talk about machine learning, we have different type of learnings which are supervised, unsupervised and reinforcement. And in many places, these three are asked about difference. And here in this video, I will give you a very simple level for example, if we talk about PK movie or the exit poll of elections then on the basis of those examples, I am going to tell the difference between these three and I am going to give their basic introduction here. So here if we start with supervised learning, then as we talk about machine learning or artificial intelligence then our main aim is how to make machines intelligent. And the main factor of making any machine intelligent is that it should learn from itself. So here we have three types of learnings supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement. These are the main three learnings we have different types of learnings also. So we are starting with supervised learnings. First I am giving you a basic technical introduction. So here in supervised learning, we have training data. What does it mean? It is known as supervised. Means here we have a supervisor. Supervisor means a teacher who is giving us some instructions. Who is training data here? Training data means we already have input, we have output also available. And on the basis of that input output, on the basis of that training data or on the basis of that labelled data, we prepare a model and in that model, we put new input and check whether the valid output is coming or not. What does it mean? If I explain it to you from a simple example then exit poll. As you must have seen that many channels had given an exit poll of this Lok Sabha election. So what is the exit poll of Lok Sabha election? It is training data. Training data means that from thousands of people from lakhs of people, they took feedback and from different category of people. It means that they took it from youngsters, from youngsters also and from children also. They took the feedback from elders. And what is the input in that feedback? And on the basis of that input, we will get so many seats for BJP Congress will get so many seats, this is the winning candidate, this is the losing candidate. So all these points have already been put into training data. And this is our training data basis, we are working on it. Means we have given this data to the machine. Now learning algorithms will work here. What learning algorithms are there? Here we use like a new base algorithm. What is the new base? It is working on supervised learning in which you have put your input and output first. Now what will happen? Now you have given new input. What is the new input here? When counting started in actual, the original data, the original people voted, when the data came and what we did with that data? On the model, the model which was based on which? The data of exit poll. The data of a dummy and the output on the basis of feedback, we made the model on the basis of that. The model means we made a prototype that this will win, this will lose, this will lose, this will have more chances of winning and this will have less chances of losing. Now what will happen? As soon as we put the new data in it or put the actual data, on the basis of that model, it gave some output. Now what is our output? If your output is correct there, means what you said in exit poll, that is your exact result. So obviously the training data you gave, that data gave a very good level of data, gave a very refined data and your algorithm is learning properly and classifying. Classification means that you have to classify the data, that it will win, it will lose, it has more chances of winning. So in this way what we do? We already provide the machine with input and output. So we call it supervised learning. If we talk about unsupervised learning, then it is a simple example, if we talk about PK movie. So when Ameer Khan comes from another planet, then he doesn't know anything, means like Newborn baby, he doesn't have any idea of what is correct, what is wrong, who is a man, who is a woman, who is a child, who doesn't know anything. So if you have seen that scene, then you must know that he doesn't know here how people's hair is. So he says that someone's hair is glued to someone's neck, someone's hair is colored. So what is this in actual? He doesn't have any knowledge, he has only input. Where is the input coming from? From the sensor, means he is looking from the eyes, he is judging from the eyes, and what is he doing on the basis of his physical structure, on the basis of his vision, what is he doing on the basis of his vision, he is doing group, he is making a cluster there. What does the cluster mean? He has seen that if someone's moustache, someone's height is good, or someone has worn a white-dressed clothes on the kurta, then that means he has kept it in the category of men, means he has kept it in the category of Jains. He has made a category of ladies, he has made a category of players, he has made a category of children, it is not that that category is absolutely right, on a first level, what did he do? He has made a group, he has made a cluster. So what is this? We have only input, not output, he doesn't know what this thing is, he is slowly learning, he is learning from himself, what is he doing? He is going towards the actual output. Here we have K-Mean, like we use the algorithm, so what do we do in K-Mean clustering? We make a group and the same type of people try to keep them in one group. So this unsupervised learning, mostly machines come in your unsupervised learning because machines have no brains, so obviously what they do? We have to give them supervised, they have to give them the first data, on the basis of that they generate some output or make some decisions. So here, if we talk about the third one, reinforcement learning. What is reinforcement learning? We have a reward and policy based on it. Reward and policy means that we have an agent, any agent like P.K. Amir Khan is an agent, that agent performed some action, where? Environment, the entire environment performed some action in it and on the basis of that action, he got either some reward, something in plus or something negative which we can call penalty and state changed something, what would be the state change? It changed something in the environment, then he made some policy on the basis of that and next time on the basis of that policy he will perform it in a different way, meaning you must have seen that scene where he gives a note of Rs. 50, and in return he gets a carrot. So what does he feel here? As soon as he performed the action, he gave a note of Rs. 50, the state changed again and what was the reward? He gave a bundle of carrots. Now what will happen? He thought that he made a policy that the picture of this person wherever it is, take it, so he takes a picture of the person from all places and gives it back to that person, as soon as he gives it back, he does not recognize it. So later on, he gives the biggest message that the value of this photo is not on the paper of only one, so what is this actual example? The reinforcement where he is making a policy on the basis of penalty and reward and while changing it in that policy, what is he doing? He learns. Generally this happens in games. You took a step in games and you got a point. You got a point in positive or a point in negative, on the basis of that you will design a policy that next time you have such a step or you should take a similar step or not. So this is basically an example. You always start from the first unsupervised learning where we don't have any knowledge, we only have input, we don't know anything. So here he makes some cluster on his basis. The cluster can be anything based on their physical structure, their color, how they look on the basis of their structure, the cluster will be made on the basis of that. Then slowly he will refine the clusters. He got feedback from other people, slowly he will refine the clusters. When the cluster was formed, now as soon as he saw a dress worn, he will know what is this, what is this, what will he put on the side, he will classify it and put it in the group of girls. Means first while doing unsupervised, he will reach in supervised learning. So this is the main difference, supervised, unsupervised and reinforcement. We use Q learning method in reinforcement. There are many algorithms. But if you implement so many algorithms, you can implement them by using Python, you can implement them on MATLAB and through R programming, you can implement them. Thank you. Thank you.
